---
title: "functional_groups"
author: "MB"
date: "2026-04-02"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Load Packages

```{r packages, results = 'hide'}
#install required packages (run once)
packages <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "stringr", "cluster", "ape", "factoextra", "adegenet", "ggplot2", "patchwork", "randomForest"
)

#check if all packages are installed, install if not
new_packages <- packages[!(packages %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])]
if(length(new_packages)) install.packages(new_packages)

#load all packages
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(cluster)
library(ape)
library(factoextra)
library(adegenet)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(randomForest)

select <- dplyr::select
filter <- dplyr::filter
mutate <- dplyr::mutate

#set options
options(prompt = "=")
```

## Data Import and Output Setup

```{r data-import}
#copy paths and update if needed
#study_data contains our study data, fish_traits contains our copy of the fishtraits database (download the current file here___), output_dir is the folder where plots/excel files generated by this script will save to
study_data <- "C:/Users/mmbet/Documents/GitHub/DGS-Patterns-of-Fish-Diversity/Objective 2/Data/Study_metadata.xlsx"
fish_traits <- "C:/Users/mmbet/Documents/GitHub/DGS-Patterns-of-Fish-Diversity/Objective 2/analyses/functional_groups/FishTraits_14.3.xls"
output_dir       <- "C:/Users/mmbet/Documents/GitHub/DGS-Patterns-of-Fish-Diversity/Objective 2/analyses/functional_groups"

#load data
study_metadata_df <- read_excel(study_data)
fish_traits_df <- read_excel(fish_traits)
```



```{r data-cleaning}
#Make sure all metadata species names are in correct format
fix_species_name <- function(name) {
  name <- str_trim(name)
  words <- str_split(name, "\\s+")[[1]]
  if (length(words) >= 2) {
    words[1] <- paste0(toupper(substr(words[1], 1, 1)), tolower(substr(words[1], 2, nchar(words[1]))))
    words[2] <- paste0(tolower(substr(words[2], 1, 1)), substr(words[2], 2, nchar(words[2])))
    return(paste(words, collapse = " "))
  } else {
    return(paste0(toupper(substr(words[1], 1, 1)), tolower(substr(words[1], 2, nchar(words[1])))))
  }
}

abbr_sp_metadata <- study_metadata_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Spp) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Spp)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Abbr_sp_metadata = sapply(Spp, fix_species_name)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(Abbr_sp_metadata) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Abbr_sp_metadata)

print(abbr_sp_metadata)

# Combine FishTraits GENUS and SPECIES into a single name, matching the format: "Genus species"
fish_traits_sp <- fish_traits_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GENUS) & !is.na(SPECIES)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    FishTraits_sp = paste0(
      toupper(substr(GENUS, 1, 1)), tolower(substr(GENUS, 2, nchar(GENUS))),
      " ",
      tolower(SPECIES)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(FishTraits_sp)

print(fish_traits_sp)

#flag species in abbr_sp_metadata not found in FishTraits
abbr_sp_metadata <- abbr_sp_metadata %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    In_FishTraits = Abbr_sp_metadata %in% fish_traits_sp$FishTraits_sp,
    Flag = ifelse(!In_FishTraits, "NOT FOUND in FishTraits", "")
  )

cat("Total species in Abbr_sp_metadata:", nrow(abbr_sp_metadata), "\n")
cat("Matched in FishTraits:", sum(abbr_sp_metadata$In_FishTraits), "\n")
cat("NOT matched in FishTraits:", sum(!abbr_sp_metadata$In_FishTraits), "\n\n")

flagged <- abbr_sp_metadata %>% dplyr::filter(Flag != "")
if (nrow(flagged) > 0) {
  cat("Flagged species (not found in FishTraits):\n")
  print(flagged)
} else {
  cat("All species matched successfully.\n")
}

# Get the matched species list
matched_species <- abbr_sp_metadata %>%
  dplyr::filter(In_FishTraits == TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::pull(Abbr_sp_metadata)

# Build FishTraits metadata for matched species
fishtraits_metadata <- fish_traits_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    FishTraits_sp = paste0(
      toupper(substr(GENUS, 1, 1)), tolower(substr(GENUS, 2, nchar(GENUS))),
      " ",
      tolower(SPECIES)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(FishTraits_sp %in% matched_species) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Combine fine sediment columns into one binary
    FINESED   = ifelse(MUCK == 1 | CLAYSILT == 1 | SAND == 1, 1, 0),
    # Combine coarse sediment columns into one binary
    COARSESED = ifelse(GRAVEL == 1 | COBBLE == 1 | BOULDER == 1 | BEDROCK == 1, 1, 0),
    # Combine debris/vegetation columns into one binary
    DEBRIS    = ifelse(VEGETAT == 1 | DEBRDETR == 1 | LWD == 1, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    FishTraits_sp,
    MAXTL, LONGEVITY, MATUAGE, FECUNDITY, 
    SLOWCURR, MODCURR, FASTCURR,
    LARGERIV, SMALLRIV, SPRGSUBT, CREEK,
    SURWCOL, BENTHIC,
    FINESED, COARSESED, DEBRIS
  )
# Replace FishTraits missing data codes with NA
fishtraits_metadata <- fishtraits_metadata %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(-FishTraits_sp, ~ ifelse(. %in% c(-555, -999, -1), NA, .)))

#check continuous variables for NAs
cat("Summary of continuous traits:\n")
print(summary(fishtraits_metadata[, c("LONGEVITY", "MATUAGE", "FECUNDITY")]))

# Report how many NAs per column after replacement
cat("Missing values per trait after replacing FishTraits codes:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(fishtraits_metadata %>% dplyr::select(-FishTraits_sp))))
print(fishtraits_metadata)
```

Compute Gower Distance and Run PCoA

```{r Gower and PCoA}
# Prepare trait matrix (species as rows, traits as columns)
# as.numeric conversion happens after NA replacement above
trait_matrix <- fishtraits_metadata %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(-FishTraits_sp, ~ as.numeric(as.character(.)))) %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames("FishTraits_sp")

# Report any remaining NAs
na_check <- colSums(is.na(trait_matrix))
cat("NA check after coercion:\n")
print(na_check)

# Define binary columns and get indices in the same chunk
binary_cols <- c("SLOWCURR", "MODCURR", "FASTCURR",
                 "LARGERIV", "SMALLRIV", "SPRGSUBT", "CREEK",
                 "SURWCOL", "BENTHIC",
                 "FINESED", "COARSESED", "DEBRIS")

binary_idx <- which(names(trait_matrix) %in% binary_cols)

# Compute Gower distance — daisy() handles NAs automatically by default
gower_dist <- daisy(trait_matrix, metric = "gower",
                    type = list(asymm = binary_idx))

# Run PCoA with Cailliez correction for negative eigenvalues
pcoa_result <- pcoa(gower_dist, correction = "cailliez")

# Extract PCoA scores
pcoa_scores <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result$vectors)
pcoa_scores$species <- rownames(pcoa_scores)

# Proportion of variance explained
eigenvalues <- pcoa_result$values$Rel_corr_eig
pcoa_var <- round(eigenvalues * 100, 1)

# --- Scree plot ---
scree_df <- data.frame(
  Axis = 1:length(pcoa_var),
  Variance = pcoa_var
)

scree_plot <- ggplot(scree_df[1:min(10, nrow(scree_df)), ], 
                     aes(x = Axis, y = Variance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  geom_point(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "PCoA Scree Plot",
       x = "PCoA Axis",
       y = "% Variance Explained") +
  theme_bw()

# Save scree plot
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "scree_plot.jpg"),
       plot = scree_plot, dpi = 300,
       width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")

scree_plot

#scree plot shows no single dominant axis, what do the axes represent?

# Extract loadings/scores
pcoa_loadings <- as.data.frame(pcoa_result$vectors)
print(head(pcoa_loadings))

# Correlate each original trait with the PCoA axes to interpret them
trait_correlations <- cor(trait_matrix, pcoa_result$vectors[, 1:4], 
                          method = "spearman",
                          use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

print(round(trait_correlations, 2))
#axis 1: hydraulic preference/water column position
#axis 2: body size/river size
#axis 3: substrate type/stream position
#axis 4: structural habitat complexity
```

Silhouette Analysis/Optimal K

```{r Silhouette and K}
# Test k from 2 to min(8, n_species - 1)
n_species <- nrow(trait_matrix)
max_k <- min(8, n_species - 1)

sil_scores <- sapply(2:max_k, function(k) {
  km <- kmeans(pcoa_scores[, 1:4], centers = k, nstart = 25, iter.max = 100)
  sil <- silhouette(km$cluster, gower_dist)
  mean(sil[, 3])
})

sil_df <- data.frame(
  k = 2:max_k,
  avg_silhouette = sil_scores
)

optimal_k <- sil_df$k[which.max(sil_df$avg_silhouette)]
cat("Optimal number of clusters:", optimal_k, "\n")

# Silhouette plot
sil_plot <- ggplot(sil_df, aes(x = k, y = avg_silhouette)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = optimal_k, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Silhouette Analysis — Optimal k",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)",
       y = "Average Silhouette Width") +
  theme_bw()

# Save silhouette plot
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "silhouette_plot.jpg"),
       plot = sil_plot, dpi = 300,
       width = 7, height = 5, units = "in")
sil_plot



```


```{r PCoA and group assignments}
# force k=5 
set.seed(42)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(pcoa_scores[, 1:4],
                        centers = 5,
                        nstart = 25,
                        iter.max = 100)

pcoa_scores$cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)

# PCoA biplot (Axis 1 vs 2)
pcoa_plot <- ggplot(pcoa_scores, aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2, color = cluster, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = cluster), type = "t", level = 0.95, alpha = 0.15, geom = "polygon") +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = cluster), type = "t", level = 0.95, linewidth = 0.7) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = species), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 30) +
  labs(title = "PCoA Biplot — Functional Groups (k=5)",
       x = paste0("PCoA Axis 1 (", pcoa_var[1], "% variance)"),
       y = paste0("PCoA Axis 2 (", pcoa_var[2], "% variance)"),
       color = "Functional Group",
       fill = "Functional Group") +
  theme_bw()

# Save PCoA plot
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "pcoa_biplot_k5.jpg"),
       plot = pcoa_plot, dpi = 300,
       width = 9, height = 7, units = "in")

pcoa_plot

# --- SPECIES GROUP ASSIGNMENTS ---
functional_groups_df <- data.frame(
  Species = rownames(trait_matrix),
  Functional_Group = kmeans_result$cluster
) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Functional_Group, Species)

print(functional_groups_df)
```

Trait Summaries and Random Forest

```{r trait summary and random forest}
# Impute NAs for summary and random forest
impute_median <- function(x) {
  x[is.na(x)] <- median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  return(x)
}

impute_mode <- function(x) {
  mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(x), decreasing = TRUE)[1]))
  x[is.na(x)] <- mode_val
  return(x)
}

trait_matrix_imputed <- trait_matrix %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(all_of(c("MAXTL", "LONGEVITY", "MATUAGE", "FECUNDITY")), impute_median)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(all_of(binary_cols), impute_mode))

# Compute mean trait values per functional group
trait_summary <- trait_matrix_imputed %>%
  mutate(Functional_Group = as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)) %>%
  group_by(Functional_Group) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ round(mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), 2))) %>%
  as.data.frame()

cat("Mean trait values per functional group:\n")
print(trait_summary)

# Save trait summary as CSV
write.csv(trait_summary, 
          file.path(output_dir, "trait_summary_k5.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

# Visualize trait summary as heatmap
trait_summary_long <- trait_summary %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Functional_Group, 
                      names_to = "Trait", 
                      values_to = "Mean_Value")

# Scale values within each trait for visualization
trait_summary_long <- trait_summary_long %>%
  group_by(Trait) %>%
  mutate(Scaled_Value = as.numeric(scale(Mean_Value))) %>%
  ungroup()

heatmap_plot <- ggplot(trait_summary_long, 
                       aes(x = Trait, y = as.factor(Functional_Group), fill = Scaled_Value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "steelblue", mid = "white", high = "firebrick",
                       midpoint = 0, name = "Scaled\nMean") +
  labs(title = "Trait Profiles by Functional Group (k=5)",
       x = "Trait",
       y = "Functional Group") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "trait_heatmap_k5.jpg"),
       plot = heatmap_plot, dpi = 300,
       width = 10, height = 5, units = "in")

heatmap_plot

# --- OPTION 2: RANDOM FOREST VARIABLE IMPORTANCE ---
set.seed(42)
rf_data <- trait_matrix_imputed %>%
  mutate(Functional_Group = as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster))

rf_result <- randomForest(Functional_Group ~ ., 
                          data = rf_data,
                          importance = TRUE, 
                          ntree = 500)

cat("Random Forest confusion matrix:\n")
print(rf_result$confusion)

# Extract and plot variable importance
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_result)) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Trait") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(MeanDecreaseAccuracy))

importance_plot <- ggplot(importance_df, 
                          aes(x = reorder(Trait, MeanDecreaseAccuracy), 
                              y = MeanDecreaseAccuracy)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Trait Importance for Functional Group Separation",
       x = "Trait",
       y = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy") +
  theme_bw()

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "rf_importance_k5.jpg"),
       plot = importance_plot, dpi = 300,
       width = 7, height = 6, units = "in")

importance_plot
```

```{r print group assingments}
# Full species list with functional group assignments and key traits
species_assignments <- trait_matrix_imputed %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Species") %>%
  mutate(Functional_Group = as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Species, Functional_Group, MAXTL, LONGEVITY, MATUAGE, FECUNDITY,
                SLOWCURR, MODCURR, FASTCURR, LARGERIV, SMALLRIV, SPRGSUBT, CREEK,
                SURWCOL, BENTHIC, FINESED, COARSESED, DEBRIS) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Functional_Group, Species)

# Print to console
cat("Species Functional Group Assignments:\n")
print(species_assignments, n = Inf)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(species_assignments,
          file.path(output_dir, "species_functional_groups_k5.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nSaved to:", file.path(output_dir, "species_functional_groups_k5.csv"), "\n")
```
